request for proposals rfp contract data analyst issued by indiana legal services, inc. ils submission deadline june 5, 2025 1. introduction indiana legal services, inc. ils is a statewide, notforprofit organization that provides free legal services to eligible clients in civil cases through eight branch offices. ils seeks proposals from qualified individuals or firms to provide data analysis services on a contract basis. the selected contractor will support ils in enhancing its use of data to improve client services, measure effectiveness, and drive systemic change. ils collects a large amounts of data as part of our application process and legal work, so we know a lot about our applicants, clients, cases, and outcomes. data about the legal issues our clients face is also available from external sources. ils wants to be able to understand, use, and visualize this data to show how our clients are affected by laws and practices. we will use this information to understand if we fairly allocating our resources to the geographic and subjectmatter areas of greatest need and to motivate decision makers to act. the data analyst will work closely with the executive team to improve indiana legal services use of data to enhance client services, improve the organizations effectiveness, and create systemic change. this is the first position of its kind at indiana legal services. 2. scope of work the selected contractor will be responsible for recommending organizationwide practice goals and measures of success. fostering a dataaware culture and supporting staff in using data to inform daily practice. designing and conducting research for special projects. improving access to innovative technologies and tools for better data collection and client services. building data and mapping tools and templates. collaborating with ils staff to investigate research questions and communicate findings in a clear, actionable manner. 3. qualifications experience applicants must meet the following minimum qualifications demonstrated commitment to the mission of indiana legal services. a babs degree and at least one year of experience in data collection and analysis using microsoft excel, tableau prep builder, tableau dashboards, or equivalent tools. experience with json, rest apis, and either python or r is preferred but not required. strong administrative and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks efficiently. ability to work independently and collaboratively. excellent written and verbal communication skills. familiarity with data sources related to poverty is a plus. 4. proposal requirements interested applicants should submit a proposal including a cover letter detailing relevant experience and proposed approach to fulfilling the scope of work. a resume or organizational profile for firms. proposed hourly rate. a list of three references, including email addresses and telephone numbers, who have worked with the bidder on similar projects or have knowledge of the bidders relevant skills and experience. 5. submission process proposals should be submitted via email to megan stuart megan.stuartilsi.net. for any questions regarding this rfp, please contact megan stuart, director of advocacy at megan.stuartilsi.net 6. evaluation criteria proposals will be evaluated based on relevant experience and qualifications. understanding of the scope of work and proposed approach. costeffectiveness of the proposed hourly rate. references and past performance. 7. contract term compensation this is a remote position. the selected contractor will enter into a contract agreement with ils. compensation will be based on the agreed hourly rate. 8. timeline rfp release date april 5, 2025 proposal submission deadline june 5, 2025 anticipated selection date july 5, 2025 project start date july 31, 2025 ils reserves the right to reject any or all proposals, negotiate specific contract terms, or cancel this rfp at any time.

company overview before we opened strive pharmacy we were unsatisfied with the current state of the pharmaceutical industry. most of the current pharmaceuticals only offer a one size fits all approach and often come with unwanted and potentially dangerous side effects. we found that through compounding we can offer a much more personalized solution to medicine. through this we have been able to help patients get the results they are seeking for their personal needs. after years of working for corporate chains we took the risk and opened strive pharmacy. we are dedicated to providing a better experience for each customer and clinic that we work with. come see the strive difference. our mission were flipping the script with personal medicine. we strive to interrupt an industry that has been closed to personalized care, accessibility, and nuance for too long. we strive to shift a reactive view of selfcare and ignite a proactive baselineshifting approach to health for all. the human element is our crux and catalyst, driving all that we do, whether were interacting with patients, providers, or practices. were here to partner with those ready for a change. more than a compounding pharmacy, were on a mission to positively disrupt healthcare as we know it. strive is about more than medicine. strive is about striving for the personal side of healthcare. as a data analyst in finance, you will play a crucial role in supporting the growth and success of our company. reporting to the manager of data analytics, you will have an opportunity to collaborate with clevel leadership and crossfunctional teams to drive financial planning and analysis initiatives. this is an exciting opportunity to contribute to the strategic decisionmaking process and be an integral part of the companys growth reshaping how people view healthcare today. responsibilities data extraction and transformation utilize sql to extract data from various sources, including databases, apis, and flat files. clean and prepare data for analysis, handling missing values, inconsistencies, and outliers. implement complex sql queries, aggregations, ctes, joins, and window functions to transform data into a usable format. develop and maintain etl pipelines using tools like dbt data build tool. data visualization create visually appealing and informative dashboards and reports using our bi tool, looker. collaborate with stakeholders to understand their data needs and design visualizations that effectively communicate key insights. develop and maintain data models and datasets within looker. data analysis conduct indepth data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies. provide actionable insights and recommendations based on data findings. support datadriven decisionmaking processes across the organization. stakeholder collaboration effectively communicate data insights to stakeholders at all levels, tailoring the information to their specific needs. build strong relationships with stakeholders to understand their data requirements and provide timely support. data governance adhere to data governance standards and best practices. contribute to the development and maintenance of data quality and security procedures. qualifications bachelors degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, or a related field. 35 years of experience in data analysis or a similar role. strong proficiency in sql, including complex query writing, aggregations, ctes, joins, and window functions. experience with data visualization tools, preferably looker. familiarity with etl processes and tools e.g., dbt is a plus. excellent analytical and problemsolving skills. strong communication and interpersonal skills. ability to work independently and as part of a team. demonstrable experience with python is preferred benefits perks strive pharmacy provides a comprehensive benefits package that encompasses various perks such as employer paid healthcare coverage available after 30 days of employment, the choice of an fsahsa, a voucher for new hire scrubs if applicable, parental leave, a 401k plan with matching contributions, and the benefit of weekends and holidays off. free compounded meds to employees and immediate family members. culture at strive, culture plays a fundamental role in shaping our workplace atmosphere. beyond our exceptional benefits package, we foster a sense of community. throughout the year, we arrange various holiday potlucks and festive celebrations. strive is committed to promoting both personal and professional development, striving for our employees to excel and grow in every aspect of their lives, both within and outside of the workplace. eeo strive pharmacy is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. we prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristics as outlined by federal, state or local laws. disclaimer please do not call the pharmacy location with questions about your application or interview. a talent acquisition partner will reach out to you.
